description: Commemorates the Last Supper of Jesus with his disciples.
Observes the crucifixion of Jesus Christ.
Celebrates the resurrection of Jesus from the dead.
Day following Easter, often observed with family gatherings.
Honors Saint George, patron saint of England.
No major holidays in April 2026; Ramadan ends in March.
Commemorates the Exodus from Egypt; includes Seders.
Holocaust Remembrance Day, honors victims of the Holocaust.
Israel Independence Day, celebrates the founding of Israel.
Celebrates the birth of Lord Hanuman, devotee of Rama.
Solar New Year; harvest festival in some regions.
Marks the Tamil New Year with family gatherings.
Auspicious day for new beginnings, buying gold.
Traditional New Year in Theravada countries; water festival.
Celebrates Buddha's birth, enlightenment, and death.
Marks the founding of the Khalsa; harvest festival.
Celebrates the second Sikh Guru's birth.
Commemorates Bahá’u’lláh's declaration as God's messenger.
Family joins Bahá’u’lláh in the Garden of Ridván.
Bahá’u’lláh leaves the Garden; end of the 12‑day festival.
